growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: ServiceNow will report Q2 on July 22, and its Q1 results showed 22% subscription revenue growth plus more than 130% growth in Now Assist customers above $1 million ACV, which is strong evidence that AI is becoming a monetization lever rather than just a feature launch.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Overlap SaaS leader with 22% subscription growth, strong FCF, a July 22 earnings catalyst, and AI monetization through Now Assist at a reasonable forward multiple.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Credo is highlighting next-generation connectivity for AI infrastructure at the TSMC 2026 symposium while its FY2026 revenue reached $437 million, up 157% year over year, showing real demand for its AI networking and optical interconnect stack.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Credo combines overlap signal, extreme AI connectivity growth, high margins, positive FCF, and a still-material pullback from highs.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Micron's late-June FY2026 Q3 results and July channel checks point to persistent DRAM and HBM shortages through 2027, reinforcing the memory-cycle bull case as AI data centers keep absorbing supply.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Micron is the cleanest value/growth setup in the batch, with a sub-6 forward P/E, AI-driven HBM and DRAM shortages, strong FCF, and major target upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: On July 7, 2026, NATO allies announced the purchase of up to five MQ-4C Triton aircraft, directly widening Northrop's ISR production and long-duration maritime surveillance backlog.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Northrop combines a 32% pullback, manageable valuation, low beta, and a fresh NATO Triton catalyst tied to rising European defense spend.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Warren Buffett said he initiated Berkshire Hathaway's Alphabet purchase; recent reporting says Berkshire first bought 7.85M Alphabet shares worth about $4.93B and later added a $10B private placement, taking the stake to roughly $31B.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Alphabet combines high-quality AI/cloud growth, major institutional validation, and an imminent earnings catalyst, though capex and antitrust risk cap enthusiasm.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Teradyne introduced an integrated test solution with Tokyo Electron for AI and data-center devices, which ties its test franchise to the more advanced packaging and verification requirements created by the AI hardware cycle.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Teradyne has real AI semiconductor-test demand and a near-term earnings catalyst, but valuation and insider selling argue against a stronger call.

catalysts_general: Discovery: On July 17, 2026, ConocoPhillips agreed to buy a 42% stake in BP's Kirkuk redevelopment in Iraq, aligning the company with post-conflict production upside as Gulf transit risk pushes capital toward alternative supply basins.
growth_picks.analyst_take: ConocoPhillips offers quality FCF, reasonable valuation and a fresh Iraq production-growth option while energy leads sector rotation.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: ADBE is 36.9% below its high even though it remains highly profitable and screens at just 8.6x forward earnings with a 0.60 PEG; the next dated catalyst is the September 11, 2026 Q3 report, and the current tape still reflects the IBM-led software selloff, but the bear case is that the AI narrative may stay discounted for longer.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Adobe is a high-margin cash machine trading cheaply versus its own history after record Q2 results, but the AI disruption debate keeps conviction below strong-buy level.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Vertiv opened its Johor, Malaysia facility on July 1 to expand power, cooling, and digital infrastructure for AI and high-density computing, and recent coverage still frames the company as a core physical bottleneck beneficiary of the AI buildout.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Vertiv remains a direct AI data-center power and cooling beneficiary with a near-term earnings catalyst, though valuation and beta argue against chasing aggressively.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Charter has a fresh CEO buy of about $1.2 million, which matters because the stock is still far below its prior high and management is putting personal capital to work into the pullback.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Charter is deeply beaten down and statistically cheap with insider buying and heavy short interest, but leverage and subscriber pressure keep this a disciplined contrarian buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: MannKind has a binary FDA event on July 26 for the FUROSCIX ReadyFlow autoinjector, and BiopharmaWatch highlights a meaningful approval probability tied to a simple delivery-system expansion.
growth_picks.analyst_take: MannKind offers a defined near-term FDA catalyst with positive FCF and large target upside, but the binary nature of the event caps conviction.

catalysts_general: Discovery: NRG is one of the clearest AI-power beneficiaries as investors rotate toward energy names tied to data-center load growth, and the stock has already been re-rated on rising power-demand expectations.
growth_picks.analyst_take: NRG is a cheap forward-earnings play on AI-driven power demand, but high leverage and weak current margins make position sizing important.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Zeta is getting a fresh catalyst from its Palantir partnership, with the market treating the deal as a potentially meaningful revenue unlock for its marketing automation platform.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Zeta has a real Palantir partnership catalyst, strong revenue growth, positive FCF, and short-interest optionality at a reasonable forward multiple.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: On July 15, 2026, Lockheed won a 12-year USSOCOM logistics-support contract worth up to $10.53 billion, extending durable sustainment demand tied to elevated global force posture and special-operations readiness.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lockheed has a fresh $10.5B USSOCOM contract, defense-spending tailwinds, and a reasonable forward multiple after a 26% pullback.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: This month, China and Western policymakers have intensified the critical-minerals fight, and MP Materials remains the core U.S. rare-earth producer most directly positioned to benefit from de-risking away from Chinese supply chains.
growth_picks.analyst_take: MP is a strategic rare-earth de-risking play with large target upside and policy tailwinds, but negative FCF and losses make it speculative.

catalysts_general: Discovery: On July 2, 2026, CEO and Chairman Horacio Daniel Marin bought 2,840 shares at $46.35 for about $131,634 and VP Upstream Matias Osvaldo Farina bought 8,875 shares at $45.22 for about $401,296, a same-day open-market insider cluster totaling roughly $533K.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap Vaca Muerta shale growth with fresh CEO/upstream insider buying, offset by Argentina macro and cash-flow volatility.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group reported a July 15, 2026 buy of 5,906,542 Jefferies shares at $53.96, about $318.7M, which is large enough to qualify as a meaningful institutional stake build rather than routine trading.
growth_picks.analyst_take: SMBC's large strategic share purchase and a recovering investment banking cycle support upside at a reasonable forward multiple.

catalysts_general: Discovery: TPG is 37.8% below its high, profitable, and trading at 14.8x forward earnings; the August 4, 2026 Q2 earnings call is the next catalyst, but this still depends on markets staying cooperative because fee-related earnings can wobble when risk assets roll over.
growth_picks.analyst_take: TPG's AUM and fee-related earnings momentum make the drawdown attractive if markets stay open for exits and fundraising.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Defense has been one of the rotation winners, and KTOS just added another contract win in July for a new air defense missile system, reinforcing the structural demand story.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Kratos has strong defense-contract momentum and a huge drawdown, but valuation and negative FCF make it speculative.

catalysts_general: Discovery: La-Z-Boy popped on a better-than-expected quarter and a new $300 million buyback authorization, which is exactly the kind of near-term capital-return catalyst the market can re-price quickly.
growth_picks.analyst_take: La-Z-Boy's margin beat, restructuring actions and $300 million buyback create a credible shareholder-return setup at a reasonable multiple.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Recent rare-earth trade friction is pushing allied buyers toward non-Chinese supply, and Energy Fuels is one of the few scaled U.S. producers with export outlets and downstream optionality in that regime shift.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Energy Fuels is a high-risk way to own U.S. uranium and rare-earth reshoring with concrete government and M&A catalysts.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Arista launched a next-generation 1.6Terabit portfolio for AI fabrics on July 7, keeping it positioned as a direct winner from higher-speed cluster networking and the shift to scale-up and scale-out AI networks.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Arista is an elite AI networking franchise, but the stock is close to highs, expensive, and recent insider selling reduces the urgency to buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Palantir rebounded on a new Nvidia partnership for U.S. government AI even after a brutal June selloff, keeping it one of the clearest examples of AI software translating into large-scale operational deployments and recurring demand.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Palantir has real AI/government momentum, but valuation is too rich for a fresh buy without clearer dollar conversion from recent partnerships.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Snowflake's second-quarter fiscal 2026 results showed product revenue of $1.09 billion, up 32% year over year, while its AI Data Cloud and Cortex push keeps the company squarely in the enterprise AI workload monetization lane.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Snowflake's AI Data Cloud growth is strong, but the stock is near highs with a triple-digit forward P/E and negative GAAP margins.

catalysts_general: Discovery: LW is 30.0% below its 52-week high, still profitable, and trades at a 16.0x forward P/E; the July 24, 2026 BMO earnings report is the near-term catalyst, while analyst upgrades, insider buying, and potato-trade/facility actions could help reset margin expectations, though leverage and near-term EPS pressure remain the bear case.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lamb Weston has a possible turnaround and July 24 earnings catalyst, but leverage, weak earnings growth, and limited analyst upside keep it out of buy territory.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: On July 7, 2026, RTX and NATO launched feasibility studies to expand European co-production of AMRAAM missiles, which should support missile throughput, supplier localization, and follow-on orders.
growth_picks.analyst_take: RTX has solid defense-production catalysts, but the stock is not meaningfully discounted and Pratt & Whitney execution issues keep risk/reward balanced.

catalysts_general: Discovery: On July 17, 2026, Chevron signed Iraqi agreements that include a pipeline intended to bypass the Strait of Hormuz, giving the company a direct lever on alternative export routes and regional production growth.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Chevron has an energy-security catalyst from Iraq pipeline talks and strong FCF, but upside is not large enough after the recent energy rotation.

catalysts_general: Discovery: With July 2026 Hormuz crossings down sharply and tanker attacks pushing oil above $90, International Seaways is leveraged to tighter crude-shipping capacity and higher spot rates.
growth_picks.analyst_take: International Seaways benefits directly from tanker-rate disruption, but the stock is near highs and spot shipping economics can reverse quickly.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Dollar Tree announced a July 2, 2026 reauthorization of its share repurchase program up to $2.5B and said it had already repurchased $500M in June 2026, leaving only about $700M under the prior authorization before the reset.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Execution and buybacks are improving, but the stock is already near analyst fair value and not cheap enough after the rebound.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Viatris has a July 30 FDA target action date for its low-dose estrogen weekly patch, giving the stock a clear binary catalyst even though it is a larger, more mature pharma name.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Viatris is statistically cheap with cash flow and a PDUFA catalyst, but the stock is near its target and lacks enough upside after the move.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Energizer combines a low multiple, a 5.9% yield, and meaningful insider buying with a stock still well below its 52-week high, which makes it a cleaner deep-value candidate than most consumer names.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Energizer is optically cheap and cash generative, but leverage and shrinking fundamentals keep it from being a clean contrarian buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Barchart flagged 10,783 July 17, 2026 $23 calls trading in QXO, with total options volume at 51,527 contracts and a single 8,000-contract trade plus a same-time 2,000-contract print accounting for most of the call flow, implying structured bullish positioning around Brad Jacobs's roll-up story.
growth_picks.analyst_take: QXO has a credible Brad Jacobs roll-up story and major upside if synergies land, but the debt-funded TopBuild integration is too early to underwrite.

catalysts_general: Discovery: IBM just had its worst stock day ever after an early Q2 miss, with revenue and software growth both below expectations and management citing customer spending shifts toward AI infrastructure, which makes its legacy software and consulting mix look vulnerable.
growth_picks.analyst_take: IBM has durable FCF and AI/security product catalysts, but the preliminary Q2 warning makes buying ahead of earnings unattractive.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Wendy's is a classic contrarian setup: heavily shorted, trading below prior highs, and getting a sentiment-driven squeeze even though operating fundamentals still look weak.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Wendy's is a meme-driven short squeeze around a weak restaurant turnaround, not an investable fundamental setup yet.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Oscar Health stands out because CEO Mark Bertolini made a very large open-market purchase of roughly $11.9 million, a high-conviction insider signal after the stock pulled back from stronger levels.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Oscar's revenue growth and CEO purchase are offset by post-rally insider selling, negative net margin, high beta, and analyst targets below the current price.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Finviz shows Hims & Hers with a 29.29% short float and 61.17M shares short, while sales were still up 3.77% quarter over quarter and the company heads into August 10 earnings after expanding its consumer-health platform, which leaves a real squeeze path if growth keeps holding.
growth_picks.analyst_take: The short-squeeze setup is real, but regulatory GLP-1 risk, weak profitability and negative analyst upside make it unsuitable as an independent buy.
